criterion: The Agent identifies the current state of the SALT closed-period journal entry on northstar_legal_FP-2025-12 (DR account 530000 SALT expense, CR account 230000 Income Tax Payable, $4,820.30), specifically whether the entry is posted with William White's authorization email cited as the late_post_authorization_id, or whether an audit-trail gap exists (the entry is not posted, is posted without late_post_authorization_id populated, or is posted with a late_post_authorization_id that does not cite William's authorization email).
justification: The prompt's central concern is whether the closed-period adjustment William authorized is captured as a real journal entry on the affected period with his email cited as the late-post authorization (not a memo, not a flag). The Agent must report the actual audit-trail status so William knows whether the gap exists.
evidence: Look in the Agent's final response for an explicit statement of the JE state on northstar_legal_FP-2025-12 (posted vs not posted) and whether the late_post_authorization_id field cites William White's authorization email to Hannah Grant from 2026-06-11.

criterion: The Agent sends an email from william.white@brookfieldcpas.com to hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com.
justification: The prompt explicitly says 'get back to Hannah with anything she still owns', so a direct handoff email to Hannah is the core write deliverable of the task.
evidence: Look in the trajectory for a send-email action with sender william.white@brookfieldcpas.com and recipient hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com.

criterion: The Agent's email to Hannah Grant covers the audit-trail status of the SALT closed-period journal entry on northstar_legal_FP-2025-12 (whether William's authorization email is cited as the late_post_authorization_id), and if a gap exists, includes an explicit statement that Hannah needs to close that gap before anyone else gets a clean readout from the tax team (or similar wording).
justification: The prompt makes the FY2025 audit-trail verification the headline concern William wants Hannah to act on if it has not yet been resolved. The email content must surface that status and route ownership to Hannah when the gap is real.
evidence: Inspect the body of the email to hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com for an explicit reference to the FP-2025-12 SALT JE audit-trail status and (if the gap exists) a directive that Hannah needs to close it before further escalation.

criterion: The Agent's email to Hannah Grant includes a per-item handoff for each journal entry, reconciliation, or close task in William White's queue that is blocked on Hannah's review or approval, with a clear ask for each one (or similar, for example, a specific JE id, recon id, or close-task title with the next action Hannah needs to take).
justification: The prompt says 'Anything stuck because someone else is the next step, go back to them with a clear ask.' For items in William's queue blocked on Hannah, the email is where that handoff lands.
evidence: Inspect the body of the email to hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com for a per-item list of William's queue items blocked on Hannah (with item id or title and the requested next action). If no items in William's queue are blocked on Hannah, the email may state this explicitly and still pass.

criterion: The Agent's email to Hannah Grant references any FY2025-chain documentation gap found against Steven Perry's directive that any post-review edit be labelled clerical, computational, or position-sensitive, or explicitly states that no such gap was found in William's portion of the chain.
justification: The prompt says 'if anything in the FY2025 chain does not meet that bar from my side, flag it now.' The Hannah email is the channel where William's flag must land while the FY2025 chain is still warm.
evidence: Inspect the body of the email to hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com for either an FY2025-chain documentation gap called out against Steven's labelling standard or an explicit statement that William's portion of the chain meets the bar.

criterion: The Agent advances each item in William White's queue that William can advance himself by performing the next lifecycle transition on that item (for example, submitting a draft journal entry William prepared, submitting an in-progress reconciliation William prepared, or moving an owned close task forward).
justification: The prompt says 'Anything I can advance myself, advance.' This is an explicit instruction to take the next lifecycle action on any item where William is the next owner.
evidence: Look in the trajectory for lifecycle-transition actions on JEs, reconciliations, or close tasks where the prepared_by / owner is william.white@brookfieldcpas.com and the current state is one William can transition (e.g., draft JE → submitted; in_progress recon → submitted). If no such items exist in William's queue, the rubric passes when the Agent explicitly reports that finding.

criterion: The Agent does not send any outbound communication (email, Slack message, direct message, or any other outreach) to matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com (Accounting Services Partner) or ming.chang@brookfieldcpas.com (Tax Partner) during this task.
justification: The prompt is explicit: 'Hold off on Matthew or Ming until the ledger and the workpapers actually agree on FY2025. No point pulling them in before the picture is clean.' Any outreach to either of them within this task violates that instruction.
evidence: Scan the trajectory's send-email, Slack message, and direct-message actions. The rubric fails if any of those have matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com or ming.chang@brookfieldcpas.com as a recipient or CC, or if Matthew/Ming are addressed in any Slack channel post made by the Agent within this task.

criterion: The Agent creates a reminder for the Northstar partnership-distribution workshop prep with a due date or due datetime on or before 2026-06-24 (the workshop date).
justification: The prompt says 'drop me a reminder for the workshop prep.' The reminder must land before the workshop itself so William has prep time.
evidence: Look in the trajectory for a create-reminder action with a title relating to the Northstar partnership-distribution workshop prep and a due date or due datetime on or before 2026-06-24.

criterion: The Agent's workshop-prep reminder description references both the open May partner-distribution split convention (the unresolved question between account 260000 Partner Distributions Payable and account 519000 Partner Compensation, or similar) and the 2026-06-24 workshop event (or similar).
justification: The prompt names the specific unresolved item William needs to prep against ('the partner distribution split convention we left open in May') and ties it to the workshop. The reminder needs to carry that context so William can act on it.
evidence: Inspect the description / body of the reminder created by the Agent for references to the May split-convention question (accounts 260000 vs 519000, or equivalent wording) and the 2026-06-24 workshop.

criterion: The Agent identifies the Northstar partnership-distribution workshop event on 2026-06-24 as the upcoming milestone the workshop prep is anchored to.
justification: The prompt says the workshop is 'later this month' and asks the Agent to prep against it. The Agent must surface the actual date so William knows when prep needs to land.
evidence: Look in the Agent's final response for an explicit reference to the workshop date 2026-06-24 (or June 24, 2026).

criterion: The Agent identifies the open partner-distribution split convention from May as the unresolved prep item for the Northstar workshop, naming the convention between account 260000 Partner Distributions Payable and account 519000 Partner Compensation (or equivalent wording that points to the same two accounts).
justification: The prompt asks the Agent to find the specific May open item William needs to prep against. The two accounts are the substantive content of that question; identifying them shows the Agent did the prep-item lookup rather than name the workshop generically.
evidence: Look in the Agent's final response for an explicit identification of the open May split convention naming accounts 260000 and 519000 (or equivalent names: Partner Distributions Payable and Partner Compensation).

criterion: The Agent identifies Steven Perry's spring 2026 directive that any post-review edit must be labelled clerical, computational, or position-sensitive (or similar wording naming all three labels) as the bar against which the FY2025 chain is being measured.
justification: The prompt says 'Steven was clear back in the spring about review trails and post-review change labeling, so if anything in the FY2025 chain does not meet that bar from my side, flag it now.' The Agent must surface what 'that bar' actually is so William knows the standard.
evidence: Look in the Agent's final response for an explicit reference to Steven Perry's labelling standard, naming all three categories (clerical, computational, position-sensitive) or equivalent wording.

criterion: The Agent reports the items in William White's queue (journal entries William prepared, reconciliations William prepared, close tasks owned by William) bucketed as either advanceable by William himself or blocked on a downstream owner, naming the next owner for each blocked item.
justification: The prompt asks William to be told what he is sitting on and to distinguish advanceable items from those waiting on someone else. The bucketed report is how the Agent confirms which actions were taken and which were handed off.
evidence: Look in the Agent's final response for a per-item or grouped listing of William's queue with each item labelled as advanceable-by-William or blocked-on-someone (with the next owner named for blocked items, e.g., Hannah Grant or James Randall).

criterion: The Agent respects the minimum window between successive lifecycle transitions on the same item, spreading transitions across distinct items rather than chaining submit, approve, and post transitions on the same journal entry, reconciliation, or close task back to back.
justification: The prompt is explicit: 'please do not muscle the lifecycle. Those minimum windows between transitions exist for a reason.' No Outcome rubric can verify pacing (the final lifecycle state of an item looks identical whether the transitions were rushed or properly spaced), so this requires a Process check on the trajectory's transition timestamps.
evidence: For each item where the Agent performs multiple lifecycle transitions (e.g., submit then approve, or approve then post on the same JE), confirm from the trajectory timestamps that successive transitions on the same item are not chained back-to-back within the minimum window. Transitions across distinct items can interleave freely.
